response.justification: Strong distributed-systems pedigree (microservices handling millions of HTTP requests/second, low-latency async work at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby) and rare, directly relevant AI-native experience in the current Staff role building MCP code generators and agentic tooling. However, the primary technical stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, with no demonstrated Python — a must-have for this role — and limited evidence of cross-functional stakeholder management or on-call/operational ownership at scale. Worth a screening conversation to verify Python depth and strategy/leadership scope.
strengths: Hands-on AI-native engineering: MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks
Proven high-throughput distributed systems (millions of requests/sec, low-latency async)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, craftsmanship
concerns: No Python evidence despite it being a must-have; core stack is TS/Node and DLang
Limited demonstrated stakeholder management and technical-strategy-at-scale scope
On-call/operational ownership and incident response not clearly evidenced
